# Limitations

## Attribution is difficult

Coordinated-looking patterns do not automatically prove deception. Fans, activists, communities, and legitimate campaigns can coordinate authentically.

## Platform data is incomplete

Researchers often lack internal ranking, recommendation, and enforcement data.

## Reporting can be contested

Named controversies should be presented carefully, especially when companies deny allegations or facts remain unresolved.

## Industry artifacts are self-interested

Vendor pages and transparency reports may be useful but are not neutral.

## Reddit/forum evidence is weak

Community posts are useful for operator language and sentiment, but should not carry high-stakes claims.

## The ethical boundary is contextual

The same technique may be legitimate or deceptive depending on disclosure, incentives, identity, scale, and intent.
